reasoning: The scroll_fire_v01 consumable is composited on a brown display pedestal at the left side of the scene. The entire display unit spans roughly 130×90px while the player character (white sparkle sprite) is approximately 32×32px — making the item presentation over 4× the player's area footprint. Consumables must be 50–75% of player size and NEVER larger. Even discounting the pedestal, the sprite element itself appears at least equal to or larger than the player. This directly triggers the 'item bigger than player' error condition. The oversized item also dominates the local scene tile area, creating a wallpaper rather than toy-diorama feel. Perspective alignment (top-down orthographic) is acceptable, but scale is the primary blocker.
